title: XLEN
description: Return the number of messages in a stream.

sections.title: Redis Software and Redis Cloud compatibility

content:
Returns the number of entries inside a stream. If the specified key does not exist the command returns zero, as if the stream was empty. However note that unlike other Redis types, zero-length streams are possible, so you should call [`TYPE`]() or [`EXISTS`]() in order to check if a key exists or not. Streams are not auto-deleted once they have no entries inside (for instance after an [`XDEL`]() call), because the stream may have consumer groups associated with it. ## Examples XADD mystream * item 1 XADD mystream * item 2 XADD mystream * item 3 XLEN mystream ## Redis Software and Redis Cloud compatibility | Redis
Software | Redis
Cloud | Notes | |:----------------------|:-----------------|:------| | ✅ Standard
✅ Active-Active | ✅ Standard
✅ Active-Active | | ## Return information **RESP2:** [Integer reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#integers): the number of entries of the stream at _key_. **RESP3:** [Integer reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#integers): the number of entries of the stream at _key_.
intro:
Returns the number of entries inside a stream. If the specified key does not
exist the command returns zero, as if the stream was empty.
However note that unlike other Redis types, zero-length streams are
possible, so you should call [`TYPE`]() or [`EXISTS`]() in order to check if
a key exists or not.

Streams are not auto-deleted once they have no entries inside (for instance
after an [`XDEL`]() call), because the stream may have consumer groups
associated with it.

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
Returns the number of entries inside a stream. If the specified key does not
exist the command returns zero, as if the stream was empty.
However note that unlike other Redis types, zero-length streams are
possible, so you should call [`TYPE`]() or [`EXISTS`]() in order to check if
a key exists or not.

Streams are not auto-deleted once they have no entries inside (for instance
after an [`XDEL`]() call), because the stream may have consumer groups
associated with it.

sections.title: Examples
sections.content:
XADD mystream * item 1
XADD mystream * item 2
XADD mystream * item 3
XLEN mystream

sections.title: Return information
sections.content:
**RESP2:**

[Integer reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#integers): the number of entries of the stream at _key_.

**RESP3:**

[Integer reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#integers): the number of entries of the stream at _key_.
